summary: End-to-end test of Revora — Revenue Recovery OS. Backend (14 pytest tests) and full frontend Playwright walkthrough all PASS. Login auto-filled credentials work, dashboard renders Indian-numbered compact INR (₹12.35 L hero, ₹9,15,000 recoverable, ₹5,35,000 outstanding), 7 ranked actions visible, AI WhatsApp + Email drafts (Claude Sonnet 4.5) generate cleanly with tone switching, mark-done strikes-through and reloads, all filters/dialogs/buttons work on Proposals/Invoices/Clients pages, mark-paid integration produces toast and refreshes status, client detail page loads with proposals/invoices/activity, logout clears token. No critical bugs.

minor.issue: allow_origins falls back to '*' from env; OK because auth uses Bearer header (allow_credentials=False), but explicit origins list would be safer in prod

issues: React dev-time hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option> — looks like Emergent visual-editor instrumentation injecting a wrapper span inside <option>. Cosmetic in dev only, but may surface as a hydration error in SSR or production. Consider using native <select> options without instrumentation OR migrating to shadcn Select to avoid the warning.

testid_spec_mismatch.note: Same for recoverable-value / outstanding-invoices-value. Functional; just docs/spec drift.

action_items: (Optional) Align data-testid names with the spec: rename value-level testids to hero-revenue-at-risk-value / hero-recoverable-value / hero-outstanding-invoices-value, OR update the spec — currently wrapper is hero-* and inner value is *-value.
(Optional) Suppress / remove the dev instrumentation that injects <span> inside <option> in new-proposal-form and new-invoice-form to fix the React hydration warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~745 lines and mixes auth, CRUD, dashboard, AI, seed logic. Consider splitting into modules (routes/auth.py, routes/proposals.py, services/ai.py, services/seed.py) before next feature wave.
compute_proposal_status / compute_invoice_status use days resolution from now_utc which can flip statuses at UTC midnight (not IST). For an India-first product consider IST-aware day boundaries.
Indexes are good (email unique, owner_id composite) — no n+1 issues; list_proposals/list_invoices preload clients_map once.
_id is correctly projected out everywhere — no ObjectId leak.
Bcrypt hashes verified to start with $2b$, login works with seeded admin; seed_admin idempotently updates password if ADMIN_PASSWORD changes.
Frontend: no error/loading boundary visible on AI draft failure path — toast only; non-blocking.
All interactive elements have data-testid (verified across Dashboard, Proposals, Invoices, Clients, Layout).
test_credentials: founder@bytehubble.com / ByteHubble@2025 (pre-seeded, pre-filled on login form). Seed produces 6 clients, 6 proposals (mix of active/cold/dead), 5 invoices (mix of due/overdue/critical/paid).
seed_data_creation: Seed runs on startup via seed_admin -> seed_demo_for_user; idempotent (skips if user has existing clients).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_revora_api.py — uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, seeded admin creds. AI draft tests have 60s timeout each; total run ~17s. Frontend testids reference: hero values are *-value (e.g. revenue-at-risk-value), action rows action-row-{id}, draft buttons draft-btn-{id}, invoice buttons mark-paid-{id} / inv-draft-{id}, client cards client-card-{id}, detail page client-detail-{id}, nav items nav-dashboard/proposals/invoices/clients, logout-btn. After navigating to Invoices/Clients pages, wait for first row/card selector before querying child action buttons — they are populated async after data fetch.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA. Initial Playwright failures (mark-paid=0, client_cards=0) were timing issues in the test script — table rows hadn't finished mounting after route change. Resolved by adding wait_for_selector on first-row/first-card before querying buttons.
